subject: Physics
chapter: Chapter 1: Exploring the Investigative World of Science

key_points: Science helps us understand the natural world through observation and experiments
Investigation in science involves asking questions and finding answers through evidence
Scientific investigations follow steps such as observation, hypothesis, experiment, and conclusion
Scientists use tools and instruments to collect accurate data
Careful observation and recording of results are important in scientific investigations
examples: Observing how plants grow in sunlight and shade
Testing which material conducts heat faster
Measuring the time taken for a ball to fall from a height
Observing changes when substances are mixed
formulas: Science investigation steps → Observation → Hypothesis → Experiment → Result → Conclusion
Basic data recording → Result = Observation + Measurement
exam_tips: Remember the main steps of scientific investigation
Always mention observation and conclusion in answers
Use real-life examples when explaining investigations
Understand the importance of experiments in science
